title: rubus glandulosus subsp. hirtus
intro: Rubus glandulosus subsp. hirtus, also known as the thorny raspberry or Himalayan raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus glandulosus plant, which is a type of bramble bush native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus glandulosus subsp. hirtus include the potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antimicrobial agent, making it a useful treatment for various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. Rubus glandulosus subsp. hirtus can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or topical cream, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and health.
